title: Physical, Service, and Performance Troubleshooting · Packet Forensics
summary: Resolve a multi-symptom campus incident across cabling, interfaces, switching, routing, addressing, wireless, and application performance. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in an evidence-backed incident resolution packet and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debug…
objectives: Diagnose wrong media, category, shielding, crosstalk, interference, attenuation, termination, polarity, and TX or RX transposition.
Interpret CRC, runts, giants, drops, errors, negotiation, and port status trends with time and traffic context.
Diagnose PoE budget or standard problems and transceiver protocol, wavelength, fiber, distance, and signal mismatches.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record
steps.instruction:
Use a changed debug case derived from this module: network troubleshooting. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Inspect the Net Cable Faults trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for diagnose wrong media, category, shielding, crosstalk, interference, attenuation, termination, polarity, and tx or rx transposition.

content.text: For Net Cable Faults, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a safe investigation headed "# Evidence: net-wireless-performance-faults-1g7fen" with a concrete hypothesis, one read-only network command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Net Wireless Performance Faults into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: net-wireless-performance-faults-1g7fen
# hypothesis: [replace with one falsifiable probable cause]
# command: choose one safe read-only simulator command
# observation: [replace with the result that supports or rejects the hypothesis]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Net Wireless Performance Faults applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Diagnose channel overlap, interference, signal loss, insufficient coverage, disassociation, roaming, and capacity.
